conversation.message: It is not great. I keep zoning out, even when I try to concentrate on something small.
Pretty much all the time, but it is worse when I am stressed or tired.
I try to bring myself back to the task, but it only works for a little while.
Maybe. If it is something simple, like folding laundry, I can manage. Anything more complex feels impossible.
I have, but I still get distracted halfway through. It is frustrating.
I get annoyed easily, even at small things like noises or interruptions.
Almost daily. I try not to show it, but it is hard to ignore.
I just push them down. I do not want to seem like I am overreacting.
They stick around. I feel on edge for hours sometimes.
I think it happens more when I am tired or already stressed about something.
Not great. I wake up in the middle of the night and cannot get back to sleep.
Probably four or five nights a week.
I usually grab my phone or just lie there thinking about stuff.
Just random thoughts, mostly about what I need to do the next day.
I feel tired and unfocused. It makes it harder to get anything done.
It is been weird. Some days I barely eat, and other days I snack constantly.
I think it depends on my mood. If I am stressed, I either forget to eat or just eat out of boredom.
Mostly junk food—crisps, biscuits, stuff like that.
Not great. I feel bloated and annoyed with myself for not eating better.
Maybe. I am not sure I would stick to it, though. I am not good at keeping routines.
I do not know. It feels a bit off, like I am not quite where I should be.
I think it is just the little things piling up—feeling tired, unfocused, and annoyed with myself.
Definitely. It is hard to stay on top of things when I feel this way.
Talking to friends sometimes helps, but I do not want to bother them with my problems too much.
Usually better, but I still worry that I am making too big a deal out of things.
Honestly, I feel sad most of the time. It’s like a weight that’s just always there.
It makes everything feel like a struggle. Even things I used to enjoy don’t make me feel any better.
I used to paint all the time, but now I just don’t have the energy to start. Even if I did, I’m not sure I’d find it fun.
It’s pretty constant. I try to get through the day, but it just feels empty.
I don’t really talk to anyone much anymore. I don’t have the energy or interest to reach out.
Not really. I try to distract myself, but it doesn’t work for long.
I’m not sure. It feels like nothing will make a difference.
I’m just tired all the time. I don’t even know what it’s like to feel rested anymore.
I can’t even get out of bed sometimes. I feel like I’m always dragging myself around, but nothing gets done.
Yeah, I start blaming myself for everything. Like, I should be doing more, but I can’t.
I just feel like I’m not good enough. If I was, I wouldn’t be so tired all the time.
Maybe, but it’s hard to separate the two. I just feel like I’m failing.
It’s bad all day, but it’s worse in the morning. I just don’t want to wake up.
I don’t know. It feels like no matter what I do, I just can’t get any energy back.
I used to love reading, but now I can’t even finish a chapter. It all just seems pointless.
I don’t even bother with most things anymore. I don’t have the motivation to do anything.
I avoid them. It just feels like there’s no point in trying.
It feels like there’s no hope. I can’t see anything getting better, and I don’t know what I’m even working toward anymore.
Yeah, it’s hard to keep my mind on anything. Even simple tasks are overwhelming.
It just makes everything harder. I can’t seem to get anything done, and then I feel worse about it.
Honestly, I don’t think it would help. It just feels like nothing will make me feel better.
I blame myself for everything. If something goes wrong, it’s my fault. Even things I can’t control.
Yeah, I just can’t. It feels like I should always do better, and when I don’t, I feel worthless.
Definitely. It makes me feel even more drained and down on myself.
I wake up really early, like 4 a.m., and then I can’t fall back to sleep.
It makes me feel even more exhausted. I don’t have the energy to do anything, and then I feel guilty for not getting things done.
Maybe. When I’m tired, everything feels worse, and I just can’t stop blaming myself.
I’ve tried, but nothing really works. I just end up wide awake, feeling terrible.
I just don’t see how things are ever going to get better. Everything feels like it’s going downhill.
Yeah, I forget to eat most of the time. I’m not really hungry, and when I do eat, I don’t enjoy it.
No, I can’t concentrate. Even things that used to be easy are hard now.
I don’t think it would make a difference. It just feels like nothing works.
Not really. I don’t think anyone would understand.
I still do them, but they do not feel as enjoyable as before.
It is hard to explain. I just do not feel happy when I am gardening or reading, even though I know I like them.
Sometimes. Like when I see a plant grow or finish a chapter, I feel okay, but it does not last long.
I have thought about trying new things, but I am not sure if it would help.
I could try that. It might make things a bit more interesting.
Okay. I will think about it.
I have not been seeing my friends much. I agree to plans, but by the time the day comes, I feel too tired to go.
Sometimes it is okay, but I usually feel drained and just want to go home.
Maybe. I feel less overwhelmed when it is just one or two people.
No, I feel bad asking for special arrangements.
I guess I could try. A coffee with one friend might feel easier than a big group dinner.
I will try to reach out to one of them.
It is not great. Some nights, I cannot fall asleep, and even when I do, I wake up feeling tired.
I just lie there thinking about random things—stuff I need to do or things I messed up.
Not really. I usually just scroll through my phone until I feel tired.
I have not, but I could give it a shot.
That sounds doable. I can try reading instead of using my phone.
Okay, I will try it.
It is harder than it should be. Even small choices, like what to eat, feel overwhelming.
I think it is the worry of making the wrong choice. I overthink everything.
Usually fine, but getting to that point is exhausting.
No, but that could help. It would feel less overwhelming than having too many choices.
I will try that. It might make things easier.
Sounds good.
It is okay, I guess. Everything just feels harder than it used to.
Like I have to put in more effort for things that should be simple, like getting up in the morning or talking to people.
A few. When I finished a gardening project, I felt good for a bit.
Yeah, but they do not last long. I go back to feeling tired or uninterested.
I will try to keep that in mind.
Thanks. I will work on that.
Sure. Last week, my laptop froze when I was editing some photos. I got so annoyed I just stopped working on it altogether.
Yeah, it kind of set the tone for the whole day. I kept snapping at little things after that.
It happens more often lately. I know these are small problems, but they feel so much bigger in the moment.
I try to take a break or go for a walk, but it does not always help.
Probably when I am tired. By the end of the day, even small annoyances get to me.
No, I have not, but it might help to see if there are patterns.
I feel like I should be doing more. I moved here to start fresh, but it feels like I am just stuck in the same place.
Building a social circle, maybe finding a better job, and feeling like I have a purpose.
Not really. I mean, I have met a few people and started exploring the city, but it does not feel like enough.
All the time. Even when I try, it feels like I should be doing more.
Like what?
I guess I could try that. It feels weird to celebrate such small things, though.
Most days, honestly. I just do not feel hungry, so I end up having something small or nothing at all.
It started a few months ago, maybe around the time I moved. I used to enjoy cooking, but now it feels like a chore.
Definitely. I feel tired and irritable when I do not eat, but even then, it is hard to force myself to eat.
A little, but I still end up skipping meals more often than not.
I have, but they do not taste the same anymore.
Maybe. It is just hard to care about meals when everything feels off.
I think I can do that.
It is like I do not know what to say. I feel out of place, even with people I have known for years.
Mostly with groups. One-on-one is easier, but even then, I feel like I say the wrong things.
I stay quiet or make up an excuse to leave early.
It lingers. I replay the conversation in my head and think about all the things I could have done differently.
Not really. I usually just focus on what went wrong.
I could try, but it is hard to notice those things in the moment.
Definitely what is going wrong. It is hard not to when it feels like I am not getting anywhere.
I guess, but it does not feel like a success since I am still struggling.
I wanted a fresh start and a chance to figure things out, but it is not going how I hoped.
I found a couple of good spots for photography, but that is about it.
I like capturing unique moments, like the way light hits a building. It feels satisfying when I get it right.
I can try, but it is hard to see those moments as progress.
It is constant. No matter how much I sleep, I always feel tired.
Yes, definitely. Just getting through my workday feels like running a marathon.
Not really—it is the same from the moment I wake up until I go to bed.
Not really. Even on weekends, I feel too drained to do much.
Yes. I used to enjoy watching movies or going out with my family, but now it just feels like too much effort.
I try to push through, but it is overwhelming. Sometimes I just sit there doing nothing.
Maybe. It is hard to say, but I guess the lack of motivation plays a role too.
I feel like I am just going through the motions, not really doing anything meaningful.
I do not know... I just see others doing so much more, and I feel like I am not good enough.
No, but I feel like they are just being polite. I do not think they are impressed with me.
Occasionally, but it does not feel genuine. I feel like I am just a cog in the machine.
I feel like I am failing them too. I do not spend enough time with them, and when I do, I am not really present.
No, but I feel like they deserve more from me.
Not really. I am afraid they might agree with me.
I just feel like I do not belong. I do not have anything to contribute, so I avoid them.
Awkward. I just sit there, not saying much, and it feels pointless.
It has gotten worse recently. I used to enjoy being around people more.
I guess I feel more disconnected now. Like I do not have the energy or interest to engage.
A bit of both. I miss feeling part of something, but being there feels exhausting.
Maybe one-on-one conversations, but even those are rare now.
Not really. I feel like they might not even notice if I do not show up.
Yes. Everything feels equally important, and I get stuck trying to figure out what to tackle first.
I usually procrastinate or ask someone else what they think.
Sometimes, but it feels like I am just pushing the problem onto someone else.
It slows me down a lot. I worry I am falling behind because I cannot make decisions quickly.
Yes, sometimes. Even small things, like what to have for dinner, can feel overwhelming.
All the time. I worry I made the wrong choice and that it will come back to bite me.
Yes. I feel like I cannot afford to mess up, even on small things.
I think the tiredness makes everything harder. It is like a chain reaction.
Sure. If I feel drained at work, I get behind, which makes me feel like a failure. Then I avoid people because I do not want to talk about it.
I feel guilty. I should be spending time with them, but I just want to be alone.
I do not know... maybe I feel like I would not be good company anyway.
Maybe, but it is hard to believe that.
I am not sure. I guess I would need to feel less tired and more like myself again.
Yes, I think so.
I feel sad most of the time, even when there is nothing specific to feel sad about. It feels like a cloud is always hanging over me.
Not really. It is just always there. I even feel like I am not contributing enough to my family or anyone.
I do not do as much as I think I should. My partner works full-time, and I barely manage to keep the house running.
They do not say anything, but I feel like they must notice. It feels like I am failing them.
Rarely. Even when I try, it does not feel like it is enough.
Sometimes, but they always come back.
No, I do not want to worry them.
Not well. I feel drained all the time, even after a full night’s sleep.
Honestly, everything. Even small things like doing the dishes or making a phone call seem impossible.
Not really. Even things I used to enjoy, like gardening, feel exhausting now.
It has been years, but it got worse after my mum passed away.
Probably. She was such a big part of my life. I have not been the same since.
Not really. I just push through, but it feels like I am carrying it everywhere.
Maybe. I am not sure if it will help, but I am willing to try.
It has been terrible. I cannot even read a book or follow a conversation without my mind wandering.
Pretty much all the time. Even when I try to focus, it feels like my brain just will not cooperate.
Yes, making decisions feels impossible. I second-guess everything and end up doing nothing.
Sometimes music helps, but not always. Mostly, I just feel stuck.
Probably. I am constantly thinking about everything I should be doing.
I have, but even that feels like too much sometimes.
That might help. I hope so.
Not really. Things that used to make me happy just feel like chores now.
I used to love baking, but now it feels pointless. I cannot even remember the last time I baked something.
Yes, I think it does. My kids ask me to do things, but I just cannot find the motivation.
Guilty. I know they miss the old me, but I do not know how to get back to that.
No, I do not want them to think I am weak.
Maybe. I would not know how to start, though.
That might be good.
I do, but it is hard to feel hopeful about it.
I just do not see things getting better. It feels like I am stuck in this rut forever.
Sometimes, but I do not think they will make a difference.
I want to, but it feels like so much effort.
I suppose. It just feels overwhelming.
I guess I could try. Maybe something like cooking dinner one night.
I just feel like I am not pulling my weight anymore. My husband works so hard, and I just stay at home. I feel like a failure.
They do not say it, but I think they must. I mean, I am not contributing financially or doing much around the house.
They would probably say I am being too hard on myself, but I do not think they understand how useless I feel.
No, I do not want to bother them with my problems. They have enough going on.
Maybe. I just do not want them to feel like they have to fix me.
I am not sure. It feels scary, but maybe it would help.
It is everything. I lost my job, I feel so tired all the time, and I do not know what to do next. I do not think I can fix any of it.
Probably the job search. I look at postings, but I feel like I am not good enough for anything. It is like I am stuck.
Not really. It feels like every time I try to think positively, I just remember all the ways I have failed.
I guess it would feel less impossible, but I do not know where to start.
Maybe. I could look into it, but I am not sure it will help.
If I break it into tiny steps, I think I could give it a shot.
It makes everything harder. Even simple things, like getting dressed or cooking, feel like a mountain I cannot climb.
A few months ago, after I lost my job. It has just gotten worse since then.
Not really. I wake up early and cannot get back to sleep. Sometimes I feel like I barely slept at all.
Not really. I try resting during the day, but I still feel so tired.
No, everything just feels like a chore now. I do not have the energy for it.
I could try, but it feels like even that might be too much.
It is like my mind just freezes. I think about all the options and worry about making the wrong choice.
I worry that I will regret it later. It sounds silly, but even something as small as choosing between a sandwich and soup feels like too much.
Yes, especially with bigger decisions. But it is worse with the small ones because I feel like I should not struggle with them.
I do not know. Maybe it would be fine, but it feels so risky.
I could try, but I am not sure it will help.
I guess it would be worth trying.
It makes me want to stay out of their way. I do not want them to see how useless I feel.
Maybe, but I do not want them to feel like they have to take care of me.
I do not know. I feel like they might not understand or think I am being dramatic.
They would probably want to help, but I do not think I deserve it.
I guess I could try telling them how tired I have been feeling lately.
I am not sure, but it might be worth a try.
Not great. I keep going over everything I have done wrong, and it feels like I cannot escape it.
My marriage. I think about all the ways I failed her. If I had just done things differently, maybe it would not have ended.
I do not know. When I think about it, all I see are the mistakes I made. It feels like I was always the problem.
Yes. It is the same everywhere. I let people down, and I do not see how I can fix anything.
All the time. It is like a constant reminder of how much I have failed. I cannot shut it off.
I feel worthless. Like there is nothing I can do to make up for everything I have done wrong.
No. I feel like no one would understand, and it just feels like something I have to deal with on my own.
It has not changed. I feel like I cannot sit still, even though I am completely exhausted.
I pace around or fidget, but it does not help. I feel like I should be doing something, but I cannot figure out what.
It is pretty much all the time, but it gets worse in the evenings. That is when my thoughts start racing.
Mostly about how I have wasted my life and how I am stuck in this place. It just keeps going in circles.
It is awful. I either cannot fall asleep, or I wake up in the middle of the night and cannot get back to sleep.
Drained. I am tired all the time, but I still cannot stop feeling like I need to move or do something.
Not really. I try to distract myself, but it does not work.
I guess, but it is hard to imagine anything making a difference.
No. Everything I used to like feels pointless now.
Playing the guitar, going for walks, even watching movies. But now, none of it feels worth it.
I cannot even remember. It has been months, at least.
Exhausting. It feels like too much effort, and I know it will not help anyway.
Yes. I avoid everyone. I do not feel like I have the energy to talk to anyone or explain how I feel.
Sometimes, but it is easier to just stay alone. Talking feels like too much work.
I do not know. It feels like nothing will change, but I guess we could try.
Terrible. I cannot concentrate on anything for more than a few minutes.
My mind just starts wandering. I get caught up in negative thoughts, and I cannot stay on track.
Yes. Even those feel impossible. I get halfway through something and just stop because I lose focus.
It makes me feel useless, like I cannot even handle the basics.
It is like this all day. There is no break from it.
Probably. It feels like everything is connected—like the restlessness, the thoughts, and the lack of focus all feed into each other.
Maybe. It is hard to believe anything will help, but I guess we can try.
Not good. I feel like I cannot do anything right. Even simple tasks feel impossible.
Everything. I feel restless but also too tired to do anything. I cannot focus, and I do not enjoy anything anymore.
I just sit there, thinking about everything I have messed up. It does not feel like there is a point in trying.
It feels pointless. I know it will not make me feel any better, so why bother?
Yes. I do not see how anything can change. It feels like this is just how things are now.
I do not know. It is hard to believe anything will make a difference, but I guess we can try.
Honestly, I feel sad most of the time. It’s like a weight that’s just always there.
It makes everything feel like a struggle. Even things I used to enjoy don’t make me feel any better.
I used to paint all the time, but now I just don’t have the energy to start. Even if I did, I’m not sure I’d find it fun.
It’s pretty constant. I try to get through the day, but it just feels empty.
I don’t really talk to anyone much anymore. I don’t have the energy or interest to reach out.
Not really. I try to distract myself, but it doesn’t work for long.
I’m not sure. It feels like nothing will make a difference.
I’m just tired all the time. I don’t even know what it’s like to feel rested anymore.
I can’t even get out of bed sometimes. I feel like I’m always dragging myself around, but nothing gets done.
Yeah, I start blaming myself for everything. Like, I should be doing more, but I can’t.
I just feel like I’m not good enough. If I was, I wouldn’t be so tired all the time.
Maybe, but it’s hard to separate the two. I just feel like I’m failing.
It’s bad all day, but it’s worse in the morning. I just don’t want to wake up.
I don’t know. It feels like no matter what I do, I just can’t get any energy back.
I used to love reading, but now I can’t even finish a chapter. It all just seems pointless.
I don’t even bother with most things anymore. I don’t have the motivation to do anything.
I avoid them. It just feels like there’s no point in trying.
It feels like there’s no hope. I can’t see anything getting better, and I don’t know what I’m even working toward anymore.
Yeah, it’s hard to keep my mind on anything. Even simple tasks are overwhelming.
It just makes everything harder. I can’t seem to get anything done, and then I feel worse about it.
Honestly, I don’t think it would help. It just feels like nothing will make me feel better.
I blame myself for everything. If something goes wrong, it’s my fault. Even things I can’t control.
Yeah, I just can’t. It feels like I should always do better, and when I don’t, I feel worthless.
Definitely. It makes me feel even more drained and down on myself.
I wake up really early, like 4 a.m., and then I can’t fall back to sleep.
It makes me feel even more exhausted. I don’t have the energy to do anything, and then I feel guilty for not getting things done.
Maybe. When I’m tired, everything feels worse, and I just can’t stop blaming myself.
I’ve tried, but nothing really works. I just end up wide awake, feeling terrible.
I just don’t see how things are ever going to get better. Everything feels like it’s going downhill.
Yeah, I forget to eat most of the time. I’m not really hungry, and when I do eat, I don’t enjoy it.
No, I can’t concentrate. Even things that used to be easy are hard now.
I don’t think it would make a difference. It just feels like nothing works.
Not really. I don’t think anyone would understand.
Sure. I do think about whether I am on the right track, especially since I just started my first job.
Not really. It is more like I wonder if I am making the most of my time.
Maybe once or twice a week, but it is usually brief.
No, not at all. I just think about it for a bit and then get back to what I am doing.
Yes, I do. I feel like I am learning and growing, even if it takes time.
No, I think I handle them pretty well.
Well, I feel proud of what I have accomplished, but I do wonder if I could manage my time better between work and hobbies.
Not really. It is more like I think, “I can do better,” and I make small changes.
Maybe once every couple of weeks when I feel a bit overwhelmed.
No, not really. I know I am doing my best, and I remind myself of that.
It definitely pushes me to improve. I try to look at it constructively.
No, I usually work through them pretty quickly.
It is like I feel I should be doing more, or I am not progressing fast enough.
Not really. It is more of a passing thought that makes me want to plan better.
Maybe once or twice a week, but it does not last long.
No, not really. I might pause for a moment to think, but then I refocus.
No, it is just a mental thing.
No, I can still enjoy spending time with friends or doing my hobbies.
It is just regular tiredness after a full day at work. Nothing unusual.
No, I can still relax or do something fun.
Not really. I think it is normal for how busy my days can be.
Yes, I usually wake up feeling fine.
No, I feel productive and energised most of the time.
Not at all. I still make time for my hobbies or meeting friends.
I would say it is positive. I feel good about where I am.
Not really. If I feel down, it is short-lived and I focus on what I can do to feel better.
No, I still enjoy my hobbies, like painting or hiking.
I feel optimistic overall. I know there is room to grow, but I am excited about it.
Occasionally, but I remind myself that I am capable and can manage things one step at a time.
I usually take a break, talk to someone, or focus on small tasks to move forward.
I think I am doing okay, but sometimes I feel like I am not doing as well as I should be.
Mostly at work. I look at others who seem to be advancing faster, and it makes me wonder if I am falling behind.
Not really. I figure it is just something I need to handle on my own.
Maybe. I think I am just too hard on myself sometimes.
I think I have been staying organised and meeting deadlines, even when it feels challenging.
I can try that. It might help me focus on the positives more.
It is fine once I get started, but getting started is harder than it used to be.
Mostly work-related things, but even hobbies sometimes feel like a chore.
I tell myself to just start small, like doing one little thing first.
It does, but on some days, I still feel like I am dragging my feet.
I have not tried that much. I usually just keep going until I am done.
Sure, I can give it a shot.
Yeah, things like people interrupting me when I am working really get on my nerves.
I usually just take a deep breath and move on. I do not want to make a big deal out of it.
It passes quickly, but I feel bad for getting annoyed in the first place.
Maybe. I guess everyone gets annoyed sometimes.
Probably. I do not take many breaks, so that might help me reset.
It is mostly fine, but sometimes I wake up feeling tired even after a full night’s sleep.
Yes, especially if I have had a busy day or if something is on my mind.
Not really. I usually just scroll through my phone until I fall asleep.
I could try that. Maybe I could read a book instead.
That sounds manageable. I will give it a try.
I have been staying on top of things at work and helping out more at home, which feels good.
A little, but I still catch myself focusing on what I could be doing better.
Comparing myself to others, mostly. I see friends achieving big things, and I feel like I should be doing more.
It would probably take some pressure off. I just need to remind myself to do that.
That could work. It would help me see what I am actually doing right.
Pretty good overall, but I have been a little tired.
I just feel like I need more time to relax, especially at night. It takes me longer to fall asleep.
Maybe 30 minutes to an hour? It is not too bad, just annoying sometimes.
Sometimes, but not always. I get through my day fine most of the time.
Most days, yes. But if I have had a late night worrying about things, then no.
Planning the wedding! It is exciting, but my brain keeps thinking about the details when I should sleep.
I try reading or using a calming app. Sometimes it helps, sometimes not.
I still enjoy them, but I do not get as excited as I used to.
Cooking, trying new recipes, even planning my wedding—it is all nice, but the spark is not the same.
I think it is just temporary. I still do them, and I like them; it is just a bit harder to feel really enthusiastic.
I guess I miss the excitement of starting something new. Now, I just feel like I am going through the motions sometimes.
Probably. There is so much to balance—it can get overwhelming.
I try, but even when I do, my mind drifts back to my to-do list.
That is a good idea. I will try to do that more intentionally.
It is silly, really. I know I am doing fine, but sometimes I just feel like I should be doing more.
Being more productive, being better organised, handling everything perfectly.
Oh, definitely. I like things to be just right.
I get frustrated with myself, even if I know it is not a big deal.
I try to remind myself it is okay, but it takes time to shake it off.
Maybe a little. I focus on what I could have done better instead of what went well.
I managed to finalise the wedding venue, which was a big deal.
Mostly fine, but there are moments where I feel a bit down.
It is hard to say. Sometimes it is just feeling like there is too much to do or thinking about how things could go wrong.
They usually pass quickly, especially if I talk to a friend or distract myself.
Not always. I do not want to sound negative or like I am complaining.
They would probably be supportive, but I still hesitate.
I think so. I do want to feel closer to them.
It is a lot, but I am trying to stay organised.
Most of the time, yes. But there are days when it feels like too much.
I try to take deep breaths or distract myself with something fun, like watching a show.
Yes, for a bit. But the stress comes back when I think about everything I need to do.
I have, but I like to be in control of the details.
That is true. I will try to ask for help more.
